title: Warm Polish Rye Crumble
description: A yeasty, crunchy comfort dish with dark brown soft sugar and a sugar‑free twist, served warm for a sweet finish.
instructions: In a bowl whisk rye flour, baking powder, and salt together.
In a separate bowl, dissolve yeast in warm milk and let sit 5 minutes until foamy.
Combine wet and dry mixtures, add melted butter, dark brown soft sugar, stevia, and erythritol crystals, and mix until dough forms.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 5 minutes until smooth.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 200°C and line a baking sheet with parchment.
Punch down the dough, roll into a log, cut into 1 cm thick slices, and arrange on the sheet.
Bake for 20 minutes until golden and crisp; let cool slightly before serving.
